1.title: The Burger Post
1.body:
I kinda like the first Iron Man movie. Robert Downey Jr. is the ultra-rich, extremely intelligent and very capable Tony Stark who is captured in the Afghanistan desert. After a miraculous invention of the iron man suit and a return to the U.S he is confronted by his assistant.
Pepper: Tony, you have to go to the hospital. The doctor has to look at you.
Tony Stark: I don't have to do anything. I've been in captivity for three months. There are two things I want to do. One, I want an American cheeseburger, and the other...
Just my thinking! Today it is time for a cheeseburger! Caveman as I think I am I googled "grass-fed burger SF" and went for the Roam Artisan Burgers located on Fillmore Street. Not that I talk from much experience, but that was the best burger I have ever had.
But of course, go big or go home. I could not stop there. In the afternoon I went for another burger with sweet potato fries and god damn! It was good. As this is really my first blog post ever and i am still testing things out I will just going show you some more pictures.
And oh yeah, if you want to know how an American burger diner looks like I even have a photo of that.
Tommorrow it is back to paleo. I think...

2.title: Walkaholic
2.body:
Whenever I visit a new city I walk a lot. Like a lot. In San Francisco that means an incredible amount of mean hills and lots of swet from the baking sun. So today I made a new concept which seamed completely new to me. No-walking-day.Therefore the day got occupied with public transportation, free WiFi-connection cafés and coffee shops with cold drinking water. The coffee shops turned out to work the best for me as I managed to sketch out a new layout for my blog. I even got my newly created c++ program running on my laptop with graphics and audio! What a God. I am truly close to becoming an ingenious game programmer. On topic i read the story God Mode in my new book "Press Start to Play". Anyways, I witnessed two new things today in the San Francisco wildlife and of course you need to know about them:
I have already become a huge fan of Roam Artisan Burgers. 100% grass-fed meat and they agree to use giant lettuce leaves instead of buns. They even have almost-paleo sweet potato fries. So I went there again today. Two times. The second time I was pretty exhausted from walking a mile in the sun, so I wanted some of those sweet sweet fries. I sat down next to this woman. My guess is she was around 28 years of age, good looking and with expensive clothes. Maybe she worked at a jewelry shop? Anyways, as I ate my 4 dollars portion of fries she wolfed down:
  • 3 large burgers
  • 3 large potato salads with extra oil on top (she asked for extra oil...)
  • A large portion of different fries
  • Around 2 large liters of 7UP soda
See my emphasis on the word large? Afterwards I could hear her puke it all up at the bathroom, and then she quickly walked away from the crime.
In another scene two very drunk guys were harrasing people on bus 28, which I of course was in. When the busdriver had had enough he simply grabbed the radio and this played out:
Busdriver: I need two drunk men removed from my bus.
PD: Roger that sir, where is your next stop?
Busdriver: Geary.
PD: We will be there.
My jaw dropped to the floor. Apparently buses have a direct line to the Police Department. Just another day in America.

3.title: Cali
3.body: Today was a long but beautiful day and I am very tired, so to sum things up i did roughly three things today:Found some dollars at the beach while watching surfers, rescue choppers and a pack of 7-8 whales 50 meters of the coast.Finally found the perfect San Francisco "feel" with a steep hill, a cable car and the perfect view of Alcatraz.And of course i found a foosball table. Tomorrow there is a DYP tournament. Let's see how it goes!

4.title: Fresno wtf?
4.body:
I figured I wanted to get a new book in San Francisco. A couple of minutes later in the bookstore I immediatly get approached by a man in his sixties who looks a bit like a well groomed santa claus. "This book is a great read" he says so of course I look confused and asks about the book. "You are not from here" he then says. How the hell did he know? I tell him a bit about myself and he immediatly concludes I would not understand the book so he walks away. Ten seconds later he shouts from across the bookstore, which is actually kinda large, that he has got another book for me. About Russian history. Thanks a lot. We talk a bit, he asks me to go on a date with one of his daughters and tries to understand why I want to go to Fresno next week. I tell him I know a girl there and I watch him make the wrong connection. "You should really go to Hawaii instead, it is really cheap from here..."

Anyways, I wanted to go to Fresno - or actually to Exeter which is a small town to the south of Fresno - because I know an old friend there and I could stay with her family for a while. So I went to San Francisco Airport and rented a car from the car company Dollar using a bunch of dollars. They gave me a Toyota Corolla; The American car of dreams. I did not take more than one minute of driving that I had just gotten the smallest car in history of America...The more south I got the more people drove pickup trucks!And of course I needed a picture of a real truck. Those things look amazing.

I got to Exeter with almost no trouble at all and was given a warm american welcome with dinner already served as I set foot inside the house.They even have the loveliest dogs! The coming days I will see if coming to Fresno was such a bad idea as folks in SF suggested, but I already have a feeling it might be worth it.

5.title: If the heat doesn't kill ya...
5.body:
I am in Exeter and it is freakin' hot! It's like being baked alive! Especially on the top of my head because some of my hair has gone missing right at that location lately... Therefore I try to stay indoors away from the sun to work on my epic C++ Space Invaders game. I am actually getting the hang of it, I think. Maybe it will be put up for download soon. Currently I am living with a nice family at the foot of the mountains in Exeter and they have been very kind to show me around, give me a room and lots of free food. They are almost too nice, so i might start getting suspicious soon.That steak must have had something in it. It made me feel way too happy. And the potatoes even tasted like sugar. Hmm...
Outside the house there is a pretty disasterous draught going on. It's hard to get water for the farms, some families have no other option than to move and on top of that there are huge forest fires that are acting very hard to put out for the firemen. Some days you can see the ash from the fire and with the draught it has a bit of a doomsday feel to it. Interstellar anyone? But still it is very exciting here. Lots and lots of farmland, an unlimited amount of pick-up trucks ( I even got to drive one! ) and beautiful nature all around. I got to see Sequoia National Park, which is a place that makes you feel kinda small. Sequoia was really something special. I even saw two small bears!
Another day I saw this big green bug flying around and I sort of got a little scared because I didn't really know if California have any dangerous wildlife. So I asked the man in the family I live with about it:
My question: "You have any dangerous animals here? Like bees and wasps and stuff?"
His answer: "Nah, not really."
That was kinda true. Except they have rattlesnakes, coyotes, mountain lions, links and black widow spiders all over the place. You litterally need to sleep with your mouth closed! They even have these guys crawling around:
Yeah i will probably die here. But it is really easy finding Jesus on the radio, so maybe that will be my salvation... I just need 29 dollars.

6.title: American Muscle
6.body: For the upcoming roadtrip on the westcoast I dreamt of ordering an American Muscle to go full throttle cruise control, but of course I had to change my mind when the webpage forwarded me to the billing page with a price tag. Hyundai was the favorable choice. Damn. I met up with my friend in San Francisco soon after picking up our wheels so we could initiate the master plan to conquer the american westcoast and Canada in a single trip. Maybe even Hawaii, who knows?As of this moment we have actually already driven a couple of hundred miles and are ready to sleep in the small town of Weed close to the dvelling volcano Mount Shasta. That thing looks really incredible so tomorrow we will get the hell outta here! Straight outta Compton!Even though our trip has just begun we've seen some amazingly beautiful sights of nature along the road already as you can see in the picture below.I am looking forward to the next couple of days where we will see the southern part of Oregon and hopefully be better at choosing the right place to get a morning coffee. Cappuccino's over here are horrible! Today we took road 17 for a couple of hours to get close to Mount Shasta. Road 17 is a really narrow road and we felt quite alone with nature and the rocks. Too bad we did not get to see much wildlife even though I tried to show off my grudging bear call.It is always nice to enter an area where the welcoming sign is holed with gunshots isn't it? Well at least they hit their target...

7.title: A Real Adventure
7.body:
Obsidian is a unique kind of ore which gets created when water and lava collides. The rapid cooling does something to the atoms and small blocks of raw obsidian ore appears ready to be collected. Obsidian ore can then be combined with various different materials to create impenetrable armors, extremely sharp swords or even beautiful interior for your luxurious housing, if you're into that. After picking up enough obsidian you might craft some Hellstone bars, forge a really awesome weapon to take down the Wall of Flesh and bring back balance in the Underworld. Just like that, easy.The thing is this: all the time I have been collection obsidian ore in Terraria, which is a 2D platform game I've played a lot, I didn't know that obsidian actually exists! To me it only existed in the game! You see, to defeat the evil forces of Terraria you have to spend hours upon hours pouring buckets of water into pools of lava to pick up bricks of obsidian using a pickaxe made of gold or a higher level material. So that is what I did. Mined up some trivial obsidian... And all that time I spend searching for a pool of lava I never even questioned what obsidian actually was.

So, on the 13th day of our USA roadtrip we stumpled upon Paulina Peak in Oregon, which apparently has an open obsidian field with a hiking trail we could walk on.It was magical to me. I got to experience Terraria in the real world! How geeky is that?! Well, real obsidian occurs when an undersurfaced volcano erupts and pushes hot lava out onto the surface of the earth, where it gets cooled off really fast by the cold air or rain. So Terraria sort of got it right! All those hours are totally worth it! At the Paulina Peak obsidian field I got to see, touch and walk on real volcano crafted obsidian which to me might be the best part of the trip yet.I spent a good amount of time walking around on top of it just because it makes a really cool sound like glass crumbling beneath you. If you know me or have read any of my other blog posts you know I like playing games, so when a real life adventure meets the virtual world I feel pretty darn excited. I even got to sit the Obsidian Throne with my "Wix Filters" cap which officially makes me the ruler of the modern world and gives me a status of totally awesome.You better do excactly as I say the next time we meet or you shall face the consequences!

8.title: The Mountain
8.body:
"Ideas are like fish. If you want to catch little fish, you can stay in the shallow water. But if you want to catch the big fish, you've got to go deeper.
Down deep, the fish are more powerful and more pure. They're huge and abstract. And they're very beautiful."
- David Lynch

I find mountains fascinating. They're threatening, magical and majestic at the same time, but also very beautiful. At scattered locations across our globe people risk everything climbing the different peaks of the earth. Fathers gamble with frostbite, avalanches and falling rocks searching for the next challenge while their daugthers can't fall asleep at home. Why the hell do they do it?

It's strange how it feels when you get some perspective. I guess we all have our own mountain to climb. Some of us are desperate to find food and shelter for the night while others are dealing with the demons inside. Some of us are bound by tradition, faith or wars while others sloth away their lives feeling miserable within. The many peaks are scattered all around us, but they are all hard to climb and most of them are obscured by mist. All mountains slowly bites away a mark on you, so which one to choose?Today is my two months anniversary for the trip to the US, and it has been quite an experience. As you might have figured out already, I've been soulsearching a bit the last few days and I think I found what I was looking for over here. I've met many new people, seen some fantastic sights of nature and witnessed the lives of ordinary people in the cities of America. I also learned a tremendous amount about myself, and some of it actually wasn't that obvious to me before. Let's just say I feel very fortunate for many things. Also I found a path that leads to what seems like my own mountain to climb. I might even have travelled the path a bit already because I can see some of the mountain now. Right now it's really steep and mysterious, but it is definitely there. And even though the peak is hidden by clouds it already seems very beautiful to me. I am excited about returning back home, which is a really nice feeling.

9.title: it itches...
9.body: If you have spent any time meditating, you probably know the feeling of your mind racing around like a wild monkey chasing a banana. And monkeys do chase after bananas. I am kinda at the point where I know what I want to do for the rest of my life, which by first thought is really comforting. It actually feels great. I cannot stop thinking about it. So today my time was mostly spent, figuring out how to do just that, what I want. That's where all the mind-racing comes from. And by the way, if you have not meditated before, try to do it first thing in the morning for just 3 minutes. Breath in deep, enjoy the day. Think about it afterwards. It is priceless. And then remember to really enjoy, what you enjoy. Good luck.
